title: Stir-Fried Vegetables with Oyster Sauce & Noodles

ingredients.title: For the noodles
items: 200g fresh egg noodles or thin wheat noodles

ingredients.title: For the stir-fry
items: 2 tbsp vegetable oil (or peanut oil for aroma)
50g snow peas or sugar snap peas

instruction.title: 1. Cook the noodles
steps: Bring a pot of water to boil. Cook noodles until just al dente. Drain and toss lightly with 1 tsp sesame oil to prevent sticking. Set aside.

instruction.title: 2. Prepare the sauce
steps: In a small bowl, mix oyster sauce, light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, sugar, water, and white pepper. Set aside.

instruction.title: 3. Stir-fry vegetables
steps: Heat a wok over high heat until smoking slightly.
Add vegetable oil. Stir-fry garlic and ginger for 15–20 seconds until fragrant.
Add carrots and broccoli first, stir-fry for 1–2 minutes.
Add bell peppers and snow peas, stir-fry another 1–2 minutes, keeping vegetables crisp-tender.

instruction.title: 4. Combine noodles and sauce
steps: Add cooked noodles to the wok.
Pour in the prepared sauce. Toss quickly using tongs or chopsticks to coat everything evenly.
Stir-fry for another 1–2 minutes to allow noodles to absorb the sauce.

instruction.title: 5. Finish and serve
steps: Garnish with sliced spring onions and toasted sesame seeds.

chefTips: High heat, fast stir-fry: Keeps vegetables crisp and bright.
Prep everything in advance: Once the wok is hot, stir-frying happens in seconds.
Use fresh oyster sauce: Gives a natural umami punch, better than the cheaper dark, thick sauces.
Noodle texture: Slightly undercook noodles so they don’t go mushy when stir-fried.
Optional protein: Add thinly sliced beef, chicken, or tofu for a more substantial dish.
